steps.title: Use h(0) = 0 to find the y-intercept
steps.content: For $h(x) = mx + b$, plugging in $x=0$ gives $b = h(0) = 0$. The y-intercept is 0, eliminating choices B and D (both add a nonzero constant).

steps.title: Use h(11) = 5 to find the slope

explanation:
• Choice A — 'h(x) = (5/11)x': h(0) = 0 ✓; h(11) = (5/11)(11) = 5 ✓. Both conditions satisfied. ✓
  • Choice B — 'h(x) = (5/11)x + 5': h(0) = 5, not 0. Fails the first condition. ✗
  • Choice C — 'h(x) = (11/5)x': h(11) = (11/5)(11) = 121/5 = 24.2, not 5. Fails the second condition. ✗
  • Choice D — 'h(x) = (11/5)x + 11': h(0) = 11, not 0. Fails the first condition. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
